   145‚Üí
   146‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset ‚Ñï} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ñï)) :
   147‚Üí    ‚àÉ m ‚àâ A, IsSidon (A ‚à™ {m}) := by

   153‚Üí    ‚àÉ m ‚â• s, m ‚àâ A ‚àß IsSidon (A ‚à™ {m}) := by
   154‚Üí  refine ‚ü®if s ‚â• 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©

   157‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by

    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíGiven a set `S` and an element `b` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite,
    31‚Üíwe define the partial density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the proportion of elements in
    32‚Üí`{x ‚àà A | x < b}` that lie in `S ‚à© A`.
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis definition was inspired from https://github.com/b-mehta/unit-fractions
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí@[inline]
    37‚Üínoncomputable abbrev partialDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    38‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) (b : Œ≤) : ‚Ñù :=
    39‚Üí  (Set.interIio (S ‚à© A) b).ncard / (Set.interIio A b).ncard
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üítheorem partialDensity_le_one {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    42‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) (b : Œ≤) : S.partialDensity A b ‚â§ 1 := by
    43‚Üí  apply div_le_one_of_le‚ÇÄ _ (Nat.cast_nonneg _)
    44‚Üí  exact mod_cast Set.ncard_le_ncard <| Set.inter_subset_inter_left _ inter_subset_right
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the upper
    48‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the limsup of the partial densities of `S`
    49‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚àû`.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def upperDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    52‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : ‚Ñù :=
    53‚Üí  atTop.limsup fun (b : Œ≤) ‚Ü¶ S.partialDensity A b
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the lower
    57‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the liminf of the partial densities of `S`
    58‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚àû`.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def lowerDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    61‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : ‚Ñù :=
    62‚Üí  atTop.liminf fun (b : Œ≤) ‚Ü¶ S.partialDensity A b
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem lowerDensity_le_one {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    65‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : S.lowerDensity A ‚â§ 1 := by
    66‚Üí  by_cases h : atTop (Œ± := Œ≤) = ‚ä•
    67‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [h, Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    68‚Üí  ¬∑ have : (atTop (Œ± := Œ≤)).NeBot := ‚ü®h‚ü©
    69‚Üí    apply Real.sSup_le (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) one_pos.le
    70‚Üí    simpa using hx.mono fun y hy ‚Ü¶ hy.trans (Set.partialDensity_le_one _ _ y)
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üítheorem lowerDensity_nonneg {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    73‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : 0 ‚â§ S.lowerDensity A := by
    74‚Üí  rw [Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]

    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí/--
    79‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `Œ≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    80‚Üídensity `Œ± : ‚Ñù` (relative to a set `A`) if the proportion of `x ‚àà S` such that `x < n`
    81‚Üíin `A` tends to `Œ±` as `n ‚Üí ‚àû`.

    83‚ÜíWhen `Œ≤ = ‚Ñï` this by default defines the natural density of a set
    84‚Üí(i.e., relative to all of `‚Ñï`).

    86‚Üídef HasDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    87‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (Œ± : ‚Ñù) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    88‚Üí  Tendsto (fun (b : Œ≤) => S.partialDensity A b) atTop (ùìù Œ±)
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/--
    91‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `Œ≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    92‚Üípositive density (relative to a set `A`) if there exists a positive `Œ± : ‚Ñù` such that
    93‚Üí`S` has density `Œ±` (relative to a set `A`).

    96‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=

   103‚Üí/-- In a non-trivial partial order with a least element, the set of all
   104‚Üíelements has density one. -/
   105‚Üí@[simp]
   106‚Üítheorem univ {Œ≤ : Type*} [PartialOrder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrder Œ≤] [OrderBot Œ≤] [Nontrivial Œ≤] :
   107‚Üí    (@Set.univ Œ≤).HasDensity 1 := by

   123‚Üí    {S T : Set Œ≤} {Œ±S Œ±T : ‚Ñù} [(atTop (Œ± := Œ≤)).NeBot] (h : S ‚äÜ T) (hS : S.HasDensity Œ±S)
   124‚Üí    (hT : T.HasDensity Œ±T) : Œ±S ‚â§ Œ±T := by
   125‚Üí  rw [HasDensity] at hS hT

   138‚Üínamespace Nat

   142‚Üí/--
   143‚ÜíThe natural density of the set of even numbers is `1 / 2`.
   144‚Üí-/
   145‚Üítheorem hasDensity_even : {n : ‚Ñï | Even n}.HasDensity (1 / 2) := by
   146‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   147‚Üí  have h {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : 1 ‚â§ n) : (({n : ‚Ñï | Even n} ‚à© Iio n).ncard : ‚Ñù) / n =
   148‚Üí      if Even n then 2‚Åª¬π else (n + 1 : ‚Ñù) /  n * 2‚Åª¬π := by

   158‚Üí  refine Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ‚ü®1, fun n hn => (h hn).symm‚ü©)

   167‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   168‚Üí  have (n : ‚Ñï) : ((S ‚à© Set.Iio n).ncard : ‚Ñù) / n ‚â§ S.ncard / n := by
   169‚Üí    by_cases h‚ÇÄ : n = 0; simp [h‚ÇÄ]

   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíA set `A` of natural numbers has logarithmic density `d` if the sequence
   191‚Üí$(1 / \log n) \cdot \sum_{k \in A, k \le n} (1/k)$ converges to `d`.

   196‚Üídef Set.HasLogDensity (A : Set ‚Ñï) (d : ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
   197‚Üí  Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï => (‚àë k ‚â§ n with k ‚àà A, (k : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π / .log n : ‚Ñù)) atTop (ùìù d)

    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- A multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An additive basis of some order. -/]
    37‚Üídef IsMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚àÉ n, A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí@[to_additive]
    40‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isMulBasis (hA : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.IsMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí@[to_additive]
    43‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_iff :
    44‚Üí    A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n ‚Üî ‚àÄ a, ‚àÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚àÄ i, f i ‚àà A), ‚àè i, f i = a := by
    45‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    46‚Üí  simp_all [IsMulBasisOfOrder]
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- No set is a multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
    49‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp) /-- No set is an additive basis of order `0`. -/]
    50‚Üílemma not_isMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Nontrivial M] : ¬¨A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 0 := by
    51‚Üí  simp [isMulBasisOfOrder_iff, eq_comm, exists_ne]
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    54‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 1 ‚Üî A = univ := by
    55‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, eq_univ_iff_forall]
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is a multiplicative basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    58‚Üí@[to_additive
    59‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an additive basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
    60‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 2 ‚Üî ‚àÄ a, a ‚àà A * A := by
    61‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if the elements that can
    64‚Üíbe expressed as a product of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/
    65‚Üí@[to_additive
    66‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if the elements that
    67‚Üícan be expressed as a sum of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/]
    68‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (A : Set M) (n : ‚Ñï) : Prop := ‚àÄ·∂Ý a in cofinite, a ‚àà A ^ n
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/-- An asymptotic multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    71‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An asymptotic additive basis of some order. -/]
    72‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚àÉ n, A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí@[to_additive]
    75‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) :
    76‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí@[to_additive]
    79‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (hA : IsMulBasisOfOrder A n) :
    80‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := .of_forall hA
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí@[to_additive]
    83‚Üílemma IsMulBasis.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : IsMulBasis A) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := by
    84‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, hn‚ü© := hA; exact ‚ü®n, hn.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder‚ü©
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    87‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚ÇÅ * ... * a‚Çô`, where each `a·µ¢ ‚àà A`. -/
    88‚Üí@[to_additive
    89‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    90‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚ÇÅ + ... + a‚Çô`, where each `a·µ¢ ‚àà A`. -/]
    91‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_prod :
    92‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚Üî ‚àÄ·∂Ý a in cofinite, ‚àÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚àÄ i, f i ‚àà A),
    93‚Üí      ‚àè i, f i = a := by
    94‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    95‚Üí  simp_all [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
    98‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    99‚Üí@[to_additive
   100‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
   101‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
   102‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff :
   103‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 2 ‚Üî ‚àÄ·∂Ý a in cofinite, a ‚àà A * A := by
   104‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   107‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.of_finite [Finite M] :
   108‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n := by simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
   111‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   112‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `0`. -/]
   113‚Üílemma not_isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Infinite M] : ¬¨IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 0 := by
   114‚Üí  simpa [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, ‚Üê not_infinite] using Set.infinite_of_finite_compl (by simp)
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí@[to_additive]
   117‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.ne_zero [Infinite M]
   118‚Üí    (hA : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n) : n ‚âÝ 0 := by rintro rfl; simp at hA
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí@[to_additive]
   121‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.nonempty [Infinite M]
   122‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.Nonempty := by
   123‚Üí  by_contra!
   124‚Üí  simp [this, IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, hA.ne_zero, finite_univ_iff,
   125‚Üí    _root_.Infinite.not_finite] at hA
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí/-- `A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/
   128‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   129‚Üí/--`A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/]
   130‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚Üî A·∂ú.Finite := by
   131‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, compl_def]
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üívariable [LinearOrder M] [LocallyFiniteOrder M] [OrderBot M]
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí@[to_additive]
   136‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.mono [CanonicallyOrderedMul M] [IsCancelMul M] (hmn : m ‚â§ n)
   137‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder m) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := by
   138‚Üí  cases finite_or_infinite M
   139‚Üí  ¬∑ simp
   140‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®a, ha‚ü© := hA.nonempty
   141‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hmn
   142‚Üí  refine ((hA.smul_set (a := a ^ n)).union <| finite_Iio <| a ^ n).subset ?_
   143‚Üí  have : a ^ n ‚Ä¢ (A ^ m)·∂ú = (a ^ n ‚Ä¢ A ^ m)·∂ú \ Iio (a ^ n) := by
   144‚Üí    ext x
   145‚Üí    constructor
   146‚Üí    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®y, hy, rfl‚ü©
   147‚Üí      simpa
   148‚Üí    ¬∑ simp only [mem_diff, mem_compl_iff, mem_smul_set, smul_eq_mul, not_exists, not_and, mem_Iio,
   149‚Üí        not_lt, le_iff_exists_mul, and_imp, forall_exists_index]
   150‚Üí      rintro hx y rfl

   152‚Üí  simp only [m.add_comm, pow_add, setOf_mem_eq, this, diff_union_self, subset_def, mem_compl_iff,
   153‚Üí    mem_union, mem_Iio]

   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`

   166‚Üí
   167‚Üívariable [SuccOrder M] [NoMaxOrder M]
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`
   170‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of elements. -/
   171‚Üí@[to_additive
   172‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic additive basis of order `1`
   173‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of consecutive naturals. -/]
   174‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff_Ici :
   175‚Üí      IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚Üî ‚àÉ a, .Ici a ‚äÜ A := by

   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí@[to_additive]
   179‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_atTop :
   180‚Üí      IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚Üî ‚àÄ·∂Ý a in atTop, a ‚àà A ^ n := by

   182‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Convolution of Functions on ‚Ñï
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the sum (`‚àó`) convolution of functions `‚Ñï ‚Üí R`.
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí## Main Definitions
    25‚Üí* `AdditiveCombinatorics.sumConv`: The sum convolution `f ‚àó g`.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí## Notation
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí* `f ‚àó g` for `sumConv f g`.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## TODO
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí* `f ‚àò g` for `diffConv f g`.

    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace AdditiveCombinatorics
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset Classical Set
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üívariable {R : Type*} [Semiring R]
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The sum convolution of two functions `f, g : ‚Ñï ‚Üí R`, also known as the Cauchy product.
    45‚Üí`(f ‚àó g) n = ‚àë_{a+b=n} f(a)g(b)`. -/
    46‚Üídef sumConv (f g : ‚Ñï ‚Üí R) (n : ‚Ñï) : R := ‚àë p ‚àà antidiagonal n, f p.1 * g p.2
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üíinfixl:70 " ‚àó " => sumConv
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The number of sum representations is the sum convolution of `A`'s indicator
    51‚Üífunction with itself: $1_A\ast 1_A(n)$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def sumRep (A : Set ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï := (ùüô_A ‚àó ùüô_A)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí@[simp]
    56‚Üílemma sumRep_def (A : Set ‚Ñï) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    57‚Üí    sumRep A n = ((antidiagonal n).filter (fun (p : ‚Ñï √ó ‚Ñï) ‚Ü¶ p.1 ‚àà A ‚àß p.2 ‚àà A)).card := by
    58‚Üí  simp only [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, mul_ite, mul_one, mul_zero]
    59‚Üí  push_cast [‚Üê ite_and, card_filter, and_comm]
    60‚Üí  congr!
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üíopen PowerSeries
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem sumRep_eq_powerSeries_coeff (A : Set ‚Ñï) (n : ‚Ñï) : (sumRep A n : ‚Ñï) =
    65‚Üí    ((PowerSeries.mk (ùüô_A)) * (PowerSeries.mk (ùüô_A)) : PowerSeries ‚Ñï).coeff n := by
    66‚Üí  simp [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, PowerSeries.coeff_mul, PowerSeries.coeff_mk]
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend AdditiveCombinatorics

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# VC dimension in a group
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the Vapnik‚ÄìChervonenkis (aka VC) dimension of a set in a group, defined as the
    23‚ÜíVC dimension of its family of translates.
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíIt also defines the VC‚Çô dimension, which has no set family analogue.
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üívariable {G : Type*} [CommGroup G] {A B : Set G} {n d d' : ‚Ñï}
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üívariable (A d) in
    33‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    34‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    35‚Üí`y s * x i ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s` for all `i ‚àà [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive
    37‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    38‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    39‚Üí`y s + x i ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s` for all `i ‚àà [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/]
    40‚Üídef HasMulVCDimAtMost : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  ‚àÄ (x : Fin (d + 1) ‚Üí G) (y : Set (Fin (d + 1)) ‚Üí G), ¬¨ ‚àÄ i s, y s * x i ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí@[to_additive]
    44‚Üílemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.mono (h : d ‚â§ d') (hd : HasMulVCDimAtMost A d) :
    45‚Üí    HasMulVCDimAtMost A d' := by
    46‚Üí  rintro x y hxy
    47‚Üí  replace h : d + 1 ‚â§ d' + 1 := by omega
    48‚Üí  exact hd (x ‚àò Fin.castLE h) (y ‚àò Set.image (Fin.castLE h)) fun i s ‚Ü¶ (hxy ..).trans <| by simp
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    51‚Üílemma hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl : HasMulVCDimAtMost A·∂ú d ‚Üî HasMulVCDimAtMost A d :=
    52‚Üí  forall_congr' fun x ‚Ü¶ (compl_involutive.toPerm.arrowCongr <| .refl _).forall_congr fun y ‚Ü¶
    53‚Üí    not_congr <| forall_congr' fun i ‚Ü¶ compl_involutive.toPerm.forall_congr <| by simp; tauto
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí@[to_additive]
    56‚Üíprotected alias ‚ü®HasMulVCDimAtMost.of_compl, HasMulVCDimAtMost.compl‚ü© := hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    59‚Üíprotected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.empty : HasMulVCDimAtMost (‚àÖ : Set G) d := by
    60‚Üí  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ‚ü®default, .univ, by simp‚ü©
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    63‚Üíprotected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.univ : HasMulVCDimAtMost (.univ : Set G) d := by
    64‚Üí  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ‚ü®default, ‚àÖ, by simp‚ü©
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    67‚Üílemma hasVCDimAtMost_smul : HasVCDimAtMost {t ‚Ä¢ A | t : G} d ‚Üî HasMulVCDimAtMost A d := by
    68‚Üí  simpa [HasVCDimAtMost, HasMulVCDimAtMost, Classical.skolem (b := fun _ ‚Ü¶ G), ‚Üê funext_iff,
    69‚Üí    Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem]
    70‚Üí    using forall_congr' fun x ‚Ü¶ (Equiv.inv _).forall_congr <| by simp
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üívariable (A d n) in
    73‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC‚Çô dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    74‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[n] √ó [d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]‚Åø` respectively such that
    75‚Üí`y s * ‚àè k, x (k, i k) ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s` for all `i ‚àà [d + 1]‚Åø`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]‚Åø`. -/
    76‚Üí@[to_additive
    77‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC‚Çô dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    78‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[n] √ó [d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]‚Åø` respectively such that
    79‚Üí`y s + ‚àë k, x (k, i k) ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s` for all `i ‚àà [d + 1]‚Åø`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]‚Åø`. -/]
    80‚Üídef HasMulVCNDimAtMost : Prop :=
    81‚Üí  ‚àÄ (x : Fin n ‚Üí Fin (d + 1) ‚Üí G) (y : Set (Fin n ‚Üí Fin (d + 1)) ‚Üí G),
    82‚Üí    ¬¨ ‚àÄ i s, y s * ‚àè k, x k (i k) ‚àà A ‚Üî i ‚àà s
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí@[to_additive]

    25‚Üí/-- The set of subset sums of a set `A ‚äÜ M`. -/
    26‚Üídef subsetSums (A : Set M) : Set M :=
    27‚Üí  {n | ‚àÉ B : Finset M, ‚ÜëB ‚äÜ A ‚àß n = ‚àë i ‚àà B, i}

    29‚Üí/-- If `A ‚äÜ B`, then `subsetSums A ‚äÜ subsetSums B`. -/

    32‚Üí  fun _ ‚ü®C, hC‚ü© => ‚ü®C, hC.1.trans h, hC.2‚ü©
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- The set of subset sums of a sequence `‚Ñï ‚Üí M`, where repetition is allowed. -/
    35‚Üídef subseqSums' (A : ‚Ñï ‚Üí M) : Set M :=

    40‚Üí/-- A set `A ‚äÜ M` is complete if every sufficiently large element of `M` is a subset sum of `A`. -/
    41‚Üídef IsAddComplete (A : Set M) : Prop :=

    44‚Üí/-- If `A ‚äÜ B` and `A` is complete, then `B` is also complete. -/

    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- A set `A ‚äÜ M` is complete if every sufficiently large element of `M` is a subset sum of `A`. -/
    51‚Üídef IsAddStronglyComplete (A : Set M) : Prop :=

    58‚Üí/-- If `A ‚äÜ B` and `A` is strongly complete, then `B` is also strongly complete. -/

    62‚Üí/-- A sequence `A` is strongly complete if `fun m => A (n + m)` is still complete for all `n`. -/
    63‚Üídef IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq (A : ‚Ñï ‚Üí M) : Prop :=

    66‚Üí/-- A strongly complete sequence is complete. -/
    67‚Üítheorem IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq.isAddComplete {A : ‚Ñï ‚Üí M}
    68‚Üí    (hA : IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq A) :

    71‚Üíopen Classical in

    73‚Üítheorem IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq.of_isAddStronglyComplete {A : ‚Ñï ‚Üí M}
    74‚Üí    (h : IsAddStronglyComplete (.range A)) : IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq A :=

    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí/-- If `A` is strongly complete and the preimage of each element is finite, then the range of `A`
    79‚Üíis strongly complete. -/
    80‚Üítheorem IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq.isAddStronglyComplete {A : ‚Ñï ‚Üí M}
    81‚Üí    (h : IsAddStronglyCompleteNatSeq A) (hA : ‚àÄ m, (A ‚Åª¬π' {m}).Finite) :

    31‚Üí/-- A sequence of natural numbers `n‚ÇÄ < n‚ÇÅ < ...` is said to have Fabry gaps if `n‚Çñ / k ‚Üí ‚àû`.
    32‚ÜíThis is the terminology adopted in [Wa01] and some other sources. -/
    33‚Üídef HasFabryGaps (n : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï) : Prop := StrictMono n ‚àß Tendsto (fun k => n k / (k : ‚Ñù)) atTop atTop
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- A sequence of natural numbers `n‚ÇÄ < n‚ÇÅ < ...` is said to have Fej√©r gaps if `‚àë' 1 / n‚Çñ < ‚àû`.
    36‚ÜíThis is the terminology adopted in [Wa01] and some other sources. -/

    39‚Üítheorem HasFejerGaps.hasFabryGaps {f : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï} (hf : HasFejerGaps f) : HasFabryGaps f := by
    40‚Üí  refine ‚ü®hf.1, tendsto_atTop_mono'

    42‚Üí      (tendsto_div_const_atTop_of_pos (two_pos (Œ± := ‚Ñù))).2 <| tendsto_inv_nhdsGT_zero.comp <|
    43‚Üí        .comp (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.2 ‚ü®?_, ?_‚ü©) <| Nat.tendsto_div_const_atTop two_ne_zero‚ü©

    24‚Üí/-! # Arithmetic Progressions
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíMain definitions:
    27‚Üí- `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï‚àû) (a d : Œ±)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the
    28‚Üí  set consisting of an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite) with first term
    29‚Üí  `a` and difference `d`. Useful for cases in which additional conditions need to be applied to
    30‚Üí  the individual terms and/or difference.
    31‚Üí- `Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï‚àû)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the set consisting
    32‚Üí  of an arithmetic progression of length `l`, for some some first term and difference.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} [AddCommMonoid Œ±]
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíA set $S$ is an arithmetic progression of length $l$ with first term $a$ and difference $d$
    39‚Üíif $S = \{a, a + d, ..., a + (l - 1)d\}$, if $l$ if finite, else $S = \{a, a + d, a + 2d, ...\}.
    40‚ÜíThis can be written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith a l a d`, where `l : ‚Ñï‚àû` may take the infinite
    41‚Üívalue `‚ä§`.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï‚àû) (a d : Œ±) : Prop :=
    44‚Üí  ENat.card s = l ‚àß s = {a + n ‚Ä¢ d | (n : ‚Ñï) (_ : n < l)}
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíA list version of `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
    48‚Üíwhen considering images under arbitrary functions.
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üídef List.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : List Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï) (a d : Œ±) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  s = (List.range l).map (fun n ‚Ü¶ a + n ‚Ä¢ d) ‚à® s = (List.range l).reverse.map (fun n ‚Ü¶ a + n ‚Ä¢ d)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üínamespace Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üívariable {s : Set Œ±} {l : ‚Ñï‚àû} {a d : Œ±}
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üítheorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : ENat.card s = l := h.1
    58‚Üítheorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s = {a + n ‚Ä¢ d | (n : ‚Ñï) (_ : n < l)} := h.2
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    61‚Üíif `s` is empty. -/
    62‚Üí@[simp]
    63‚Üítheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d ‚Üî s = ‚àÖ := by simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    66‚Üíif `s` is a singleton. -/
    67‚Üí@[simp]
    68‚Üítheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d ‚Üî s = {a} := by simp +contextual [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üínamespace List.IsAPOfLengthWith
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üívariable {s : List Œ±} {l : ‚Ñï} {a d : Œ±}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üítheorem length (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s.length = l := by
    77‚Üí  cases h <;> simp_all
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    80‚Üíif `s` is empty. -/
    81‚Üí@[simp]
    82‚Üítheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d ‚Üî s = [] := by
    83‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    86‚Üíif `s` is a singleton. -/
    87‚Üí@[simp]
    88‚Üítheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d ‚Üî s = [a] := by
    89‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üíend List.IsAPOfLengthWith
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- In an abelian additive group `Œ±`, the set `{a, b}` with `a ‚âÝ b` is an arithmetic progression of
    94‚Üílength `2` with first term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/
    95‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthWith_pair {Œ± : Type*} [DecidableEq Œ±] [AddCommGroup Œ±]
    96‚Üí    {a b : Œ±} (hab : a ‚âÝ b) :
    97‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLengthWith {a, b} 2 a (b - a) := by
    98‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    99‚Üí  rw [Finset.card_insert_of_notMem (by simpa only [Finset.mem_singleton])]
   100‚Üí  simp
   101‚Üí  refine Set.ext fun x => ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ ?_, fun ‚ü®n, ‚ü®_, _‚ü©‚ü© ‚Ü¶ by interval_cases n <;> simp_all‚ü©
   102‚Üí  cases h with
   103‚Üí  | inl hl => use 0; simp [hl]
   104‚Üí  | inr hr => exact ‚ü®1, by norm_num, by simp_all‚ü©
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí-- Formalisation note: separate result needed for `‚Ñï` since this is not covered by
   107‚Üí-- the `AddCommGroup` result above.
   108‚Üí/-- The set `{a, b} : Set ‚Ñï` with `a < b` is an arithmetic progression of length `2` with
   109‚Üífirst term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/
   110‚Üítheorem Nat.isAPOfLengthWith_pair {a b : ‚Ñï} (hab : a < b) :
   111‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLengthWith {a, b} 2 a (b - a) := by
   112‚Üí  let ‚ü®n, h‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hab
   113‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthWith, h, add_assoc]
   114‚Üí  exact Set.ext fun x => ‚ü®fun a => by aesop, fun ‚ü®w, ‚ü®_, _‚ü©‚ü© => by interval_cases w <;> simp_all‚ü©
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí/--
   117‚ÜíThe predicate that a set `s` is an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite).
   118‚ÜíThis predicate does not assert a specific value for the first term or the difference of the
   119‚Üíarithmetic progression.
   120‚Üí-/
   121‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï‚àû) : Prop :=
   122‚Üí  ‚àÉ a d : Œ±, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí/--
   125‚ÜíA list version of `Set.IsAPOfLength`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
   126‚Üíwhen considering images under arbitrary functions.
   127‚Üí-/
   128‚Üídef List.IsAPOfLength (s : List Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
   129‚Üí  ‚àÉ a d : Œ±, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üínamespace Set.IsAPOfLength
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üíopen Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üívariable {s : Set Œ±} {l : ‚Ñï‚àû}
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üítheorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : ENat.card s = l := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üítheorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : ‚àÉ a d : Œ±, s = {a + n ‚Ä¢ d | (n : ‚Ñï) (_ : n < l)} :=
   140‚Üí  ‚ü®h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.2‚ü©
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí/-- Only the empty set is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/
   143‚Üí@[simp] theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLength 0 ‚Üî s = ‚àÖ := by simp [Set.IsAPOfLength]
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/
   146‚Üí@[simp] theorem one : s.IsAPOfLength 1 ‚Üî ‚àÉ a, s = {a} := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí/-- If a set is an arithmetic progression of lengths `l‚ÇÅ` and `l‚ÇÇ`, then the lengths are
   149‚Üíequal. -/
   150‚Üítheorem congr {s : Set Œ±} {l‚ÇÅ l‚ÇÇ : ‚Ñï‚àû}
   151‚Üí    (h‚ÇÅ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚ÇÅ) (h‚ÇÇ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚ÇÇ) :
   152‚Üí    l‚ÇÅ = l‚ÇÇ := by
   153‚Üí  rw [‚Üê h‚ÇÅ.card, h‚ÇÇ.card]
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üíend Set.IsAPOfLength
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üínamespace List.IsAPOfLength
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üíopen List.IsAPOfLengthWith
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üívariable {s : List Œ±} {l : ‚Ñï}
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üítheorem length (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : s.length = l := by
   164‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®_, _, h‚ü© := h
   165‚Üí  exact h.length
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí/-- Only the empty list is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/
   168‚Üí@[simp] theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLength 0 ‚Üî s = [] := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/
   171‚Üí@[simp] theorem one : s.IsAPOfLength 1 ‚Üî ‚àÉ a, s = [a] := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí/-- If a list is an arithmetic progression of lengths `l‚ÇÅ` and `l‚ÇÇ`, then the lengths are
   174‚Üíequal. -/
   175‚Üítheorem congr {s : List Œ±} {l‚ÇÅ l‚ÇÇ : ‚Ñï}
   176‚Üí    (h‚ÇÅ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚ÇÅ) (h‚ÇÇ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚ÇÇ) :
   177‚Üí    l‚ÇÅ = l‚ÇÇ := by
   178‚Üí  rw [‚Üê h‚ÇÅ.length, h‚ÇÇ.length]
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üíend List.IsAPOfLength
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLength_pair {Œ± : Type*} [DecidableEq Œ±] [AddCommGroup Œ±] {a b : Œ±} (hab : a ‚âÝ b) :
   183‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=
   184‚Üí  ‚ü®a, b - a, Set.isAPOfLengthWith_pair hab‚ü©
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üítheorem Nat.isAPOfLength_pair {a b : ‚Ñï} (hab : a < b) :
   187‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=
   188‚Üí  ‚ü®a, b - a, Nat.isAPOfLengthWith_pair hab‚ü©
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí/-- The empty set is not an arithmetic progression of positive length. -/
   191‚Üítheorem Set.not_isAPOfLength_empty {l : ‚Ñï‚àû} (hl : 0 < l) :
   192‚Üí    ¬¨Set.IsAPOfLength (‚àÖ : Set Œ±) l :=
   193‚Üí  fun h ‚Ü¶ by simp_all [h.congr <| Set.IsAPOfLength.zero.2 rfl]
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí/-- We say that a set `s` is free of arithmetic progressions of length `l` if `s` contains no
   196‚Üínon-trivial arithmetic progressions of length `l`. Written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthFree s l`. --/
   197‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLengthFree (s : Set Œ±) (l : ‚Ñï‚àû) : Prop :=
   198‚Üí  ‚àÄ t ‚äÜ s, t.IsAPOfLength l ‚Üí l ‚â§ 1
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí/-- Any set is free of arithmetic progressions of length `1`, because such APs are all trivial. -/
   201‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_one (s : Set Œ±) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 1 := by
   202‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree]
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí/-- Any set is free of arithmetic progressions of length `0`, because such APs are all trivial. -/
   205‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_zero (s : Set Œ±) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 0 := by
   206‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree]
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí/-- Any non-trivial arithmetic progression cannot be free of arithmetic progressions. -/
   209‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLength.not_isAPOfLengthFree {s : Set Œ±} {l : ‚Ñï‚àû}
   210‚Üí    (hs : s.IsAPOfLength l) (hl : 1 < l) : ¬¨s.IsAPOfLengthFree l := by
   211‚Üí  simpa [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree] using ‚ü®s, le_rfl, ‚ü®hs, hl‚ü©‚ü©
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí/--
   214‚ÜíDefine the largest possible size of a subset of $\{1, \dots, N\}$ that does not contain
   215‚Üíany non-trivial $k$-term arithmetic progression.
   216‚Üí-/
   217‚Üínoncomputable def Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard (k : ‚Ñï) (N : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
   218‚Üí  sSup {Finset.card S | (S) (_ : S ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N) (_ : (S : Set ‚Ñï).IsAPOfLengthFree k)}
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard_zero (N : ‚Ñï) : maxCard 0 N = N := by
   221‚Üí  simp only [maxCard, Nat.cast_zero, isAPOfLengthFree_zero, exists_const, exists_prop]
   222‚Üí  apply IsGreatest.csSup_eq
   223‚Üí  refine ‚ü®‚ü®Finset.Icc 1 N, Subset.rfl, Nat.card_Icc _ _‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
   224‚Üí  rintro n ‚ü®S, hS, rfl‚ü©
   225‚Üí  exact S.card_mono hS |>.trans_eq (Nat.card_Icc _ _)
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard_one (N : ‚Ñï) : maxCard 1 N = N := by
   228‚Üí  nth_rw 2 [‚Üê maxCard_zero N]
   229‚Üí  simp [maxCard, isAPOfLengthFree_one, isAPOfLengthFree_zero]
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí/-- A set `A` contains an arithmetic progression of length `k` with difference `d`. -/
   232‚Üídef Set.ContainsAP (A : Set Œ±) (k : ‚Ñï) (d : Œ±) : Prop :=
   233‚Üí  ‚àÉ a, ‚àÉ s, s ‚äÜ A ‚àß s.IsAPOfLengthWith (k : ‚Ñï‚àû) a d
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üídef ContainsMonoAPofLength {Œ∫ : Type} [Finite Œ∫] {M : Set Œ±}
   236‚Üí    (coloring : M ‚Üí Œ∫) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
   237‚Üí  ‚àÉ c : Œ∫, ‚àÉ ap : Set M, ((¬∑.1) '' ap).IsAPOfLength k ‚àß
   238‚Üí    ‚àÄ m ‚àà ap, coloring m = c
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí/--
   241‚ÜíA function `f : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤` has a monotone `k`-term arithmetic progression if there exists an
   242‚Üíarithmetic progression `l` of length `k` in `Œ±` such that its image under `f` is sorted.
   243‚Üí-/
   244‚Üídef HasMonotoneAP {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] (f : Œ± ‚Üí Œ≤) (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
   245‚Üí  ‚àÉ l : List Œ±, l.IsAPOfLength k ‚àß (l.map f).Pairwise (¬∑ < ¬∑)
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí/--
   248‚ÜíDefine the largest possible size of a subset of a finset `s` that does not contain
   249‚Üíany non-trivial `k`-term arithmetic progression.
   250‚Üí-/
   251‚Üínoncomputable def Finset.maxAPFreeCard (k : ‚Ñï) (s : Finset Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   252‚Üí  (s.powerset.filter fun t : Finset Œ± ‚Ü¶ (t : Set Œ±).IsAPOfLengthFree k).sup Finset.card
   253‚Üí

    22‚Üí/--
    23‚ÜíA covering system of a semiring `R` is a finite set of cosets of non-zero ideals whose union
    24‚Üícovers the whole ring. In the case where `R = ‚Ñ§`, this corresponds to a choice of finitely many
    25‚Üícongruence relations such that every integer satisfies at least one of the relations.
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üístructure CoveringSystem (R : Type*) [CommSemiring R] where
    28‚Üí  Œπ : Type
    29‚Üí  [fintypeIndex : Fintype Œπ]
    30‚Üí  residue : Œπ ‚Üí R
    31‚Üí  moduli : Œπ ‚Üí Ideal R
    32‚Üí  unionCovers : ‚ãÉ i, ({residue i} : Set R) + (moduli i : Set R) = @Set.univ R
    33‚Üí  non_trivial : ‚àÄ i, moduli i ‚âÝ ‚ä•
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--

    37‚Üía different ideal (or number).
    38‚Üí
    39‚ÜíNote: this corresponds to the notion of a covering system that Erdos was using in
    40‚Üí[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    41‚Üí-/
    42‚Üístructure StrictCoveringSystem (R : Type*) [CommSemiring R] extends CoveringSystem R where

    54‚Üí  c.unionCovers
    55‚Üí

    19‚Üí/--
    20‚ÜíA perfect difference set modulo `n` is a set `D` such that the map `(a, b) ‚Ü¶ a - b` from
    21‚Üí`D.offDiag` to `{x : ZMod n | x ‚âÝ 0}` is a bijection.
    22‚Üí-/
    23‚Üídef IsPerfectDifferenceSet (D : Set ‚Ñï) (n : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
    24‚Üí  D.offDiag.BijOn (fun (a, b) => (a - b : ZMod n)) {x : ZMod n | x ‚âÝ 0}

    22‚Üídef Triplewise {Œ± : Type*} (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop) : Prop :=
    23‚Üí  ‚àÄ ‚¶Éi j k ‚¶Ñ, i ‚âÝ j ‚Üí j ‚âÝ k ‚Üí i ‚âÝ k ‚Üí r i j k
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üínamespace Set
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} {r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop} {s t : Set Œ±} {x y z : Œ±}
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíThe ternary relation `r` holds triplewise on the set `s`
    31‚Üíif `r x y z` for all *distinct* `x y z ‚àà s`.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üíprotected def Triplewise (s : Set Œ±) (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop) : Prop :=
    34‚Üí  ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà s ‚Üí ‚àÄ ‚¶Éy‚¶Ñ, y ‚àà s ‚Üí ‚àÄ ‚¶Éz‚¶Ñ, z ‚àà s ‚Üí x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí y ‚âÝ z ‚Üí x ‚âÝ z ‚Üí r x y z
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíprotected theorem Triplewise.eq (hs : s.Triplewise r)
    37‚Üí    (hx : x ‚àà s) (hy : y ‚àà s) (hz : z ‚àà s) (h : ¬¨r x y z) :
    38‚Üí    x = y ‚à® y = z ‚à® x = z := by
    39‚Üí  have := (h <| hs hx hy hz ¬∑ ¬∑ ¬∑)
    40‚Üí  tauto
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üítheorem Triplewise.mono (h : t ‚äÜ s) (hs : s.Triplewise r) :
    43‚Üí    t.Triplewise r :=
    44‚Üí  fun _ hx _ hy _ hz hxy hyz hxz ‚Ü¶ hs (h hx) (h hy) (h hz) hxy hyz hxz
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí@[simp]
    47‚Üítheorem Triplewise_univ_iff :
    48‚Üí    Set.univ.Triplewise r ‚Üî Triplewise r := by
    49‚Üí  simp [Set.Triplewise, Triplewise]
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üítheorem triplewise_of_encard_lt (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop)
    52‚Üí    (h : s.encard < 3) : s.Triplewise r := by
    53‚Üí  unfold Set.Triplewise
    54‚Üí  contrapose! h
    55‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®x, hx, y, hy, z, hz, hxy, hyz, hxz, _‚ü© := h
    56‚Üí  trans encard {x, y, z}
    57‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [Set.encard_insert_of_notMem, *]
    58‚Üí    norm_num
    59‚Üí  ¬∑ exact encard_le_encard_of_injOn (by simp [MapsTo, hx, hy, hz]) (injOn_id _)
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí@[simp]
    62‚Üítheorem triplewise_empty (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop) :
    63‚Üí    Set.Triplewise ‚àÖ r :=
    64‚Üí  (notMem_empty ¬∑ ¬∑ |>.elim)
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí@[simp]
    67‚Üítheorem triplewise_singleton (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop) :
    68‚Üí    Set.Triplewise {x} r := by
    69‚Üí  apply triplewise_of_encard_lt
    70‚Üí  simp
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí@[simp]
    73‚Üítheorem triplewise_pair
    74‚Üí    (r : Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Œ± ‚Üí Prop) : Set.Triplewise {x, y} r := by
    75‚Üí  apply triplewise_of_encard_lt
    76‚Üí  apply (encard_insert_le {y} x).trans_lt
    77‚Üí  simp
    78‚Üí  norm_num
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üítheorem triplewise_insert :
    81‚Üí    (insert x s).Triplewise r ‚Üî s.Triplewise r ‚àß
    82‚Üí    ‚àÄ y ‚àà s, ‚àÄ z ‚àà s, x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí x ‚âÝ z ‚Üí y ‚âÝ z ‚Üí r x y z ‚àß r y x z ‚àß r y z x := by
    83‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun hxs ‚Ü¶ ?_, fun ‚ü®hs, hxs‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©
    84‚Üí  ¬∑ refine ‚ü®hxs.mono (subset_insert x s), fun y hy z hz hxy hxz hyz ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©‚ü©
    85‚Üí    ¬∑ exact hxs (mem_insert x s) (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz) hxy hyz hxz
    86‚Üí    ¬∑ exact hxs (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert x s) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz) hxy.symm hxz hyz
    87‚Üí    ¬∑ exact hxs (mem_insert_of_mem x hy) (mem_insert_of_mem x hz)
    88‚Üí        (mem_insert x s) hyz hxz.symm hxy.symm
    89‚Üí  ¬∑ intro w hw y hy z hz hwy hyz hwz
    90‚Üí    rw [mem_insert_iff] at hw hy hz
    91‚Üí    obtain rfl | hw := hw
    92‚Üí    ¬∑ refine (hxs y ?_ z ?_ hwy hwz hyz).1
    93‚Üí      ¬∑ simpa [hwy.symm] using hy
    94‚Üí      ¬∑ simpa [hwz.symm] using hz
    95‚Üí    obtain rfl | hy := hy
    96‚Üí    ¬∑ exact (hxs w hw z (by simpa [hyz.symm] using hz) hwy.symm hyz hwz).2.1
    97‚Üí    obtain rfl | hz := hz
    98‚Üí    ¬∑ exact (hxs w hw y hy hwz.symm hyz.symm hwy).2.2
    99‚Üí    exact hs hw hy hz hwy hyz hwz
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üítheorem triplewise_insert_of_not_mem (hx : x ‚àâ s) :
   102‚Üí    (insert x s).Triplewise r ‚Üî s.Triplewise r ‚àß
   103‚Üí    ‚àÄ y ‚àà s, ‚àÄ z ‚àà s, y ‚âÝ z ‚Üí r x y z ‚àß r y x z ‚àß r y z x := by
   104‚Üí  refine Set.triplewise_insert.trans <| and_congr_right' <| forall‚ÇÑ_congr ?_
   105‚Üí  intro y hy z hz
   106‚Üí  simp [(ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hy hx).symm, (ne_of_mem_of_not_mem hz hx).symm]
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üíprotected theorem Triplewise.insert (hs : s.Triplewise r)
   109‚Üí    (h : ‚àÄ y ‚àà s, ‚àÄ z ‚àà s, x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí x ‚âÝ z ‚Üí y ‚âÝ z ‚Üí r x y z ‚àß r y x z ‚àß r y z x) :
   110‚Üí    (insert x s).Triplewise r :=
   111‚Üí  Set.triplewise_insert.mpr ‚ü®hs, h‚ü©
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üíprotected theorem Triplewise.insert_of_not_mem
   114‚Üí    (hx : x ‚àâ s) (hs : s.Triplewise r)
   115‚Üí    (h : ‚àÄ y ‚àà s, ‚àÄ z ‚àà s, y ‚âÝ z ‚Üí r x y z ‚àß r y x z ‚àß r y z x) :
   116‚Üí    (insert x s).Triplewise r :=
   117‚Üí  (Set.triplewise_insert_of_not_mem hx).mpr ‚ü®hs, h‚ü©
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üítheorem triplewise_set_pred_iff {S : Set Œ±} {P : Set Œ± ‚Üí Prop} :
   120‚Üí    S.Triplewise (P {¬∑, ¬∑, ¬∑}) ‚Üî ‚àÄ T ‚äÜ S, T.ncard = 3 ‚Üí P T := by
   121‚Üí  constructor
   122‚Üí  ¬∑ intro h T hT T_ncard
   123‚Üí    obtain ‚ü®x, y, z, hxy, hxz, hyz, rfl‚ü© := ncard_eq_three.mp T_ncard
   124‚Üí    simp_rw [insert_subset_iff, singleton_subset_iff] at hT
   125‚Üí    exact h hT.1 hT.2.1 hT.2.2 hxy hyz hxz
   126‚Üí  ¬∑ intro h x hx y hy z hz hxy hyz hxz
   127‚Üí    refine h _ ?_ (ncard_eq_three.mpr ‚ü®x, y, z, hxy, hxz, hyz, rfl‚ü©)
   128‚Üí    simp [insert_subset_iff, hx, hy, hz]
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üíend Set

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 1
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1)
    23‚Üí-/
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üíopen Filter
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos1
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/--
    32‚ÜíA finite set of naturals $A$ is said to be a sum-distinct set for $N \in \mathbb{N}$ if
    33‚Üí$A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ and the sums $\sum_{a\in S}a$ are distinct for all $S\subseteq A$
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üíabbrev IsSumDistinctSet (A : Finset ‚Ñï) (N : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
    36‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N ‚àß (fun (‚ü®S, _‚ü© : A.powerset) => S.sum id).Injective
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/--
    39‚ÜíIf $A\subseteq\{1, ..., N\}$ with $|A| = n$ is such that the subset sums $\sum_{a\in S}a$ are
    40‚Üídistinct for all $S\subseteq A$ then
    41‚Üí$$
    42‚Üí  N \gg 2 ^ n.
    43‚Üí$$
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    46‚Üítheorem erdos_1 : ‚àÉ C > (0 : ‚Ñù), ‚àÄ (N : ‚Ñï) (A : Finset ‚Ñï) (_ : IsSumDistinctSet A N),
    47‚Üí    N ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üí C * 2 ^ A.card < N := by
    48‚Üí  sorry
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/--
    51‚ÜíThe trivial lower bound is $N \gg 2^n / n$.
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí@[category undergraduate, AMS 5 11]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_1.variants.weaker : ‚àÉ C > (0 : ‚Ñù), ‚àÄ (N : ‚Ñï) (A : Finset ‚Ñï)
    55‚Üí    (_ : IsSumDistinctSet A N), N ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üí C * 2 ^ A.card / A.card < N := by
    56‚Üí  sorry
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/--
    59‚ÜíErd≈ës and Moser [Er56] proved
    60‚Üí$$
    61‚Üí  N \geq (\tfrac{1}{4} - o(1)) \frac{2^n}{\sqrt{n}}.
    62‚Üí$$
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí[Er56] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems and results in additive number theory_. Colloque sur la Th\'{E}orie des Nombres, Bruxelles, 1955 (1956), 127-137.
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    67‚Üítheorem erdos_1.variants.lb : ‚àÉ (o : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù) (_ : o =o[atTop] (1 : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù)),
    68‚Üí    ‚àÄ (N : ‚Ñï) (A : Finset ‚Ñï) (h : IsSumDistinctSet A N),
    69‚Üí      (1 / 4 - o A.card) * 2 ^ A.card / (A.card : ‚Ñù).sqrt ‚â§ N := by
    70‚Üí  sorry
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí/--
    73‚ÜíA number of improvements of the constant $\frac{1}{4}$ have been given, with the current
    74‚Üírecord $\sqrt{2 / \pi}$ first provied in unpublished work of Elkies and Gleason.
    75‚Üí-/
    76‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    77‚Üítheorem erdos_1.variants.lb_strong : ‚àÉ (o : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù) (_ : o =o[atTop] (1 : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù)),
    78‚Üí    ‚àÄ (N : ‚Ñï) (A : Finset ‚Ñï) (h : IsSumDistinctSet A N),
    79‚Üí      (‚àö(2 / œÄ) - o A.card) * 2 ^ A.card / (A.card : ‚Ñù).sqrt ‚â§ N := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí/--
    83‚ÜíA finite set of real numbers is said to be sum-distinct if all the subset sums differ by
    84‚Üíat least $1$.
    85‚Üí-/
    86‚Üíabbrev IsSumDistinctRealSet (A : Finset ‚Ñù) (N : ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
    87‚Üí  ‚ÜëA ‚äÜ Set.Ioc (0 : ‚Ñù) N ‚àß (A.powerset : Set (Finset ‚Ñù)).Pairwise fun S‚ÇÅ S‚ÇÇ =>
    88‚Üí    1 ‚â§ dist (S‚ÇÅ.sum id) (S‚ÇÇ.sum id)
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/--
    91‚ÜíA generalisation of the problem to sets $A \subseteq (0, N]$ of real numbers, such that the subset
    92‚Üísums all differ by at least $1$ is proposed in [Er73] and [ErGr80].
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí[Er73] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems and results on combinatorial number theory_. A survey of combinatorial theory (Proc. Internat. Sympos., Colorado State Univ., Fort Collins, Colo., 1971) (1973), 117-138.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., _Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number theory_. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    97‚Üí-/
    98‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    99‚Üítheorem erdos_1.variants.real : ‚àÉ C > (0 : ‚Ñù), ‚àÄ (N : ‚Ñï) (A : Finset ‚Ñù)
   100‚Üí    (_ : IsSumDistinctRealSet A N), N ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üí C * 2 ^ A.card < N := by
   101‚Üí  sorry
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí/--
   104‚ÜíThe minimal value of $N$ such that there exists a sum-distinct set with three
   105‚Üíelements is $4$.
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üíhttps://oeis.org/A276661
   108‚Üí-/
   109‚Üí@[category undergraduate, AMS 5 11]
   110‚Üítheorem erdos_1.variants.least_N_3 :
   111‚Üí    IsLeast { N | ‚àÉ A, IsSumDistinctSet A N ‚àß A.card = 3 } 4 := by
   112‚Üí  refine ‚ü®‚ü®{1, 2, 4}, ?_‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
   113‚Üí  ¬∑ simp
   114‚Üí    refine ‚ü®by decide, ?_‚ü©
   115‚Üí    let P := Finset.powerset {1, 2, 4}

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 1000

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1000](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1000)

Let $A = \{n_1 < n_2 < \cdots\}$ be an infinite sequence of integers, and let $\varphi_A(k)$
count the number of $1 \leq m \leq n_k$ such that the fraction $m/n_k$ does not have
denominator $n_j$ for $j < k$ when written in lowest form; equivalently,
$n_k / \gcd(m, n_k) \neq n_j$ for all $1 \leq j < k$.

Is there a sequence $A$ such that
$$\lim_{N \to \infty} \frac{1}{N} \sum_{k \leq N} \frac{\varphi_A(k)}{n_k} = 0?$$

Erd≈ës believed no such sequence exists. This was solved by Haight [Ha] who
proved that such a sequence does exist (contrary to Erd≈ës' expectations).

[Er64b] Erd≈ës, P., _Some problems in number theory_. 1964.

[Ha] Haight, J. A., _A linear programming problem_. 1979.
-/

open Finset Filter

namespace Erdos1000

/-- For a strictly increasing sequence $a : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ of positive integers,
$\varphi_A(k)$ counts the number of $1 \leq m \leq a(k)$ such that
$a(k) / \gcd(m, a(k)) \neq a(j)$ for all $j < k$. -/
noncomputable def phiA (a : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï) (k : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
  ((Finset.Icc 1 (a k)).filter (fun m =>
    ‚àÄ j < k, (a k) / Nat.gcd m (a k) ‚âÝ a j)).card
